slides.heading: The hidden vault `.git`
slides.body:
Git snapshots your work in a **repository** — a vault stored in a hidden `.git` folder.

| Command | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| `git init` | Tell Git to watch this folder and build `.git` here |
bullets: .git keeps history — delete it only if you mean to discard the project ledger

slides.heading: Run it locally
slides.body:
In the simulated terminal beside this lesson:

```bash
git init
ls
```

You should see the repository initialise and `.git/` appear logically in Git state (Visualizer tracks the simulated repo).
bullets: Green output means Git accepted your command
